problem.title: Walls and Gates
problem.description: Given an m×n grid initialized with three possible values: -1 (a wall or obstacle), 0 (a gate), and 2147483647 (INF, representing an empty room), fill each empty room with the distance to its nearest gate. If it is impossible to reach a gate, leave the value as INF.
assumptions: Movement is allowed in 4 directions (up, down, left, right).

example.explanation: Each empty room is filled with the minimum number of steps to reach a gate (0).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Multi-Source BFS [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Enqueue all gates simultaneously and run BFS outward from all of them at once. Each cell is visited at most once, so the first time BFS reaches an empty room it is guaranteed to be via the shortest path from the nearest gate. This eliminates the need for per-room individual BFS calls and achieves optimal O(mn) time.

complexity.time: O(mn) — each cell is enqueued and processed at most once
complexity.space: O(mn) — queue can hold up to all cells

example_walkthrough.text: Grid has gates at (0,2) and (3,0). Both are enqueued. BFS spreads: neighbors of gates get distance 1, their neighbors get distance 2, and so on. Because all gates expand simultaneously, each room is labeled with the true minimum gate distance the first time it is reached.
